title: App.css - Global Stylesheets
content:
## App.css - Global Stylesheets

This file contains the global CSS variables and base styles for the DocMed application. It sets up the core color palette, typography, and layout primitives used throughout the application.

### Purpose

- **Define Design Tokens**: Establishes a consistent set of CSS variables for colors, spacing, and dimensions, ensuring a unified look and feel.
- **Base Styles**: Provides fundamental styling for the `body` element, including font family, background, and text color.
- **Layout Structure**: Defines the basic flexbox layout for the application's main structure, including the sidebar and main content area.
- **Component Base Styles**: Includes foundational styles for common UI elements like tab bars, sidebars, search inputs, navigation items, file trees, and markdown content. These styles are often augmented by component-specific CSS.

### Key Styles and Variables

**Root Variables (`:root`)**: These are the primary design tokens.

- `--bg`: Primary background color.
- `--surface`: Secondary background color, often used for cards or panels.
- `--surface-2`: A darker variant of `--surface`.
- `--border`: Primary border color.
- `--border-2`: A darker variant of `--border`.
- `--text`: Primary text color.
- `--text-2`: Secondary text color, for less emphasized text.
- `--text-3`: Tertiary text color, for muted or disabled text.
- `--accent`: The primary accent color, used for interactive elements and highlights.
- `--accent-dim`: A dim variant of the accent color.
- `--sidebar-w`: Width of the sidebar.
- `--radius`: Border radius for elements.

**Global Styles**: Applied to the `body` and overall layout.

- `body`: Sets font, background, text color, and basic `line-height`.
- `.layout`: Establishes a flex container for the main application layout (sidebar + main content).
- `.main-column`: A flex column to contain the tab bar and main content.

**Component-Specific Styles**: These are base styles that can be extended by individual components.

- `.tab-bar`, `.tab-list`, `.tab`, `.tab-close`, `.tab-add`: Styles for the tab navigation.
- `.sidebar`, `.sidebar.collapsed`, `.sidebar-top`, `.repo-title`, `.collapse-btn`, `.sidebar-body`: Styles for the collapsible sidebar.
- `.search-wrap`, `.search-icon`, `.search-input`, `.search-clear`: Styles for the search functionality within the sidebar.
- `.nav`, `.nav-item`, `.nav-badge`, `.nav-section-label`, `.nav-empty`: Styles for navigation lists and sections.
- `.tree-level`, `.tree-row`, `.tree-folder-row`, `.tree-chevron`, `.tree-icon`, `.tree-name`, `.tree-children`: Styles for the file tree component.
- `.main`: The main content area, enabling scrolling.
- `.page`, `.page-title`, `.page-subtitle`: Basic styling for content pages.
- `.stats-row`, `.stat-card`, `.stat-value`, `.stat-label`: Styles for displaying statistics.
- `.section-title`, `.file-grid`, `.file-card`, `.file-card-name`, `.file-card-path`, `.file-card-title`: Styles for home page sections and file cards.
- `.commit-list`, `.commit-item`, `.commit-sha`, `.commit-body`, `.commit-msg`, `.commit-meta`: Styles for displaying commit information.
- `.doc-filepath`: Styling for the file path displayed on documentation pages.
- `.markdown-body`: Comprehensive styles for rendering Markdown content, including headings, paragraphs, links, code blocks, lists, blockquotes, and tables.
- `.empty-state`, `.loading-pulse`: Styles for placeholder and loading states.

### Usage

This file is automatically imported by the main application entry point (`App.tsx` or similar) and its styles apply globally. Developers should leverage the defined CSS variables for consistency when creating new components or modifying existing ones. For example:

```css
.my-custom-component {
  background-color: var(--surface);
  color: var(--text-2);
  border: 1px solid var(--border);
  border-radius: var(--radius);
}
```

When building UI elements, ensure they conform to the established color palette and spacing defined in the `:root` variables for a cohesive user experience.
